picks.rationale: Mercury Systems has surged +127% over one year and +48.91% YTD, with +21.23% in the past month, driven by a strong backlog and a P/E of 42.71; news sentiment is positive at 0.138, and the company is a key beneficiary of rising missile and radar demand.

Rolls-Royce has rallied +57% over one year and +17.77% YTD, with +21.77% in the past month, driven by strong defence engine demand and a low P/E of 20.43; news sentiment is positive at 0.242, and it benefits from UK/European defence spending tailwinds.

Red Cat Holdings has gained +63% over one year and +21.40% YTD, with +24.52% in the past month, reflecting strong drone demand; news sentiment is positive at 0.284, and it is a key player in the growing autonomous systems market.

RTX has +31% one-year return and +5.80% in the past month, with a record $271 billion backlog and strong defence demand; news sentiment is positive at 0.14, and it benefits from capacity expansion and missile production push in Europe/Ukraine.

HEICO has +7% one-year return and +14.65% in the past month, with a P/E of 60.1 and strong aftermarket demand; news sentiment is positive at 0.27, and it benefits from commercial aerospace recovery and defence electronics.

TransDigm has -9% one-year return but +12.06% in the past month, with a P/E of 41.39 and strong aerospace aftermarket; news sentiment is positive at 0.244, and it benefits from defence and commercial aerospace demand.

Leonardo DRS has +31.92% YTD and +6.90% in the past month, with a P/E of 42.71 and strong defence electronics demand; news sentiment is positive at 0.343, and it benefits from growing defence budgets.
SpaceX has +13.23% YTD and +13.23% in the past month, with strong space demand and positive news sentiment at 0.267; it benefits from the space launch and satellite market.
SAIC has +1.68% YTD and +6.43% in the past month, with a low P/E of 11.49 and strong cyber/intel demand; news sentiment is positive at 0.249, and it benefits from government IT spending.
QinetiQ has -12% one-year return but +5.98% in the past month, with a P/E of 21.99 and positive news sentiment at 0.296; it benefits from UK defence spending and cyber/space demand.
